items.briefing:
A data breach has impacted 122 Australian schools and universities, affecting millions of people in what has been described as a "significant failure." Staff and students at affected institutions should watch for suspicious emails or contact from unknown parties using their personal details. Separately, AI is being used increasingly to craft convincing phishing emails that are harder to spot — meaning even well-written, personalised messages should be treated with caution.

**Practical tip:** If you receive an unexpected email asking you to click a link or provide information, verify the request by contacting the sender directly through a known phone number or official website — not by replying to the email.
